notes:
bóp = to squeeze something with your hand, pressing it to make it smaller or to push something out. It’s physical and hands-on.

You use it with soft things or parts of the body: bóp tay, bóp chân, bóp vai, bóp cò (pull/squeeze a trigger), bóp phanh (squeeze the brake). It often implies pressure with fingers or palm.

Close words: vắt (squeeze liquid out, like clothes or fruit) and ép (press/force, often stronger or more mechanical).  
It can also mean “to strangle” or “to suffocate” in a stronger sense (bóp cổ, bóp nghẹt).

translation.en: Don't squeeze the water bottle too hard.

example.vi: Tôi bóp quả cam để lấy nước.
example.es: Aprieto la naranja para sacar jugo.
example.en: I squeeze the orange to get juice.

definitions.es: Comprimir o apretar algo con fuerza.
definitions.vi: Nén hoặc bóp chặt một cái gì đó.
definitions.en: To compress or squeeze something tightly.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Can you squeeze this orange to get the juice?
When my shoulder hurts, I usually ask my friend to squeeze it gently.
Don't squeeze too hard or the bottle might break.
notes_md:
**bóp** = to squeeze something with your hand, pressing it to make it smaller or to push something out. It’s physical and hands-on.

You use it with soft things or parts of the body: bóp tay, bóp chân, bóp vai, bóp cò (pull/squeeze a trigger), bóp phanh (squeeze the brake). It often implies pressure with fingers or palm.


Close words: **vắt** (squeeze liquid out, like clothes or fruit) and **ép** (press/force, often stronger or more mechanical).  
It can also mean “to strangle” or “to suffocate” in a stronger sense (bóp cổ, bóp nghẹt).

translation.en: Squeeze the orange to get juice.

exampleSentences: Em có thể bóp trái cam này để lấy nước không?
Khi đau vai, anh thường nhờ bạn bóp nhẹ cho đỡ mỏi.
Chị đừng bóp quá mạnh kẻo chai nước bị vỡ nhé.
